channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Questions to help you get 'financially naked' with your partner
item.description: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.content:encoded: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems
item.description: As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.
item.content:encoded:
As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.

(Image credit: Charlie Riedel)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Tax refunds are trending a bit higher this year. Here's how people are spending them
item.description: Some people are splurging. Others are finding that their refunds are being swallowed up by the rising cost of gas.
item.content:encoded:
Some people are splurging. Others are finding that their refunds are being swallowed up by the rising cost of gas.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: What's got private credit investors spooked?
item.description: A private credit fund was just hit with a massive request from investors to pull some of their money out. We talk to a retiree who's already done that and explain what's got so many investors spooked.
item.content:encoded: A private credit fund was just hit with a massive request from investors to pull some of their money out. We talk to a retiree who's already done that and explain what's got so many investors spooked.
item.dc:creator: Vito Emanuel

item.title: The labor market springs back to life in March as employers add 178,000 jobs
item.description: The U.S. job market perked up last month as employers added 178,000 jobs. The unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%, mainly because the number of people seeking work declined.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. job market perked up last month as employers added 178,000 jobs. The unemployment rate dipped to 4.3%, mainly because the number of people seeking work declined.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Verdicts against Meta and Google may bring a new era of big tech accountability
item.description: Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.
item.content:encoded:
Advocates hope recent verdicts against social media platforms will build momentum for bigger changes in Silicon Valley.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: What's next for Meta in the wake of trial losses and layoffs?
item.description: The company behind Facebook and Instagram has lost two major court cases and appears to be scaling back on the virtual reality Metaverse.
item.content:encoded:
The company behind Facebook and Instagram has lost two major court cases and appears to be scaling back on the virtual reality Metaverse.

(Image credit: Tony Avelar)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Entertainment and California regulators push back against Warner-Paramount merger
item.description: California officials and Hollywood figures are pushing back on a proposed $111 billion Warner Bros.-Paramount merger, citing antitrust concerns, job risks, and media consolidation.
item.content:encoded: California officials and Hollywood figures are pushing back on a proposed $111 billion Warner Bros.-Paramount merger, citing antitrust concerns, job risks, and media consolidation.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Trump administration sues three states over attempts to regulate prediction markets
item.description: The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.
item.content:encoded:
The suits are the most ambitious effort to date that the Trump administration has gone to try to override state laws and set the rules for the fast-growing and increasingly divisive betting industry.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Have Trump's tariffs worked? This is where things stand a year after 'Liberation Day'
item.description: It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.
item.content:encoded:
It has been a year since President Trump announced double-digit tariffs on imports from around the world. So far, those levies have not produced the economic boom the president promised.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
